title: ilex subcordata
intro: Ilex subcordata, also known as the Hooker's pepper or Oregon holly, is a species of flowering plant in the family Aquifoliaceae, native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Ilex subcordata include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to support immune function and reduce stress. In herbal preparations, Ilex subcordata is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
